siteFooter.tagline: A seeded voxel world protocol on Solana.

hero.title: A world is fair only when players can verify the rules.
hero.subtitle: NiceChunk is designed around public seed rules, reproducible results, versioned upgrades, wallet-owned actions, and clear trust boundaries between the client, Guardians, and Solana.

principles.title: Fairness is a system design constraint, not a slogan.
principles.body: The project should be judged by whether players can inspect the rules, reproduce key outputs, and see which parts are live, planned, or still client-only.

deterministic.title: Deterministic generation
deterministic.body: A seed, chunk coordinate, and generation version should produce the same terrain result for every verifier.

reproducible.title: Reproducible rolls
reproducible.body: Resource discovery should be derived from public inputs whenever possible, instead of hidden server randomness.

versioned.title: Versioned rules
versioned.body: UI changes, world-rule changes, economy-rule changes, and chain-state changes must be tracked separately.

boundary.title: Trust boundaries
boundary.body: The website must say what is client-only, what Guardians can assist with, and what is intended to be verified on-chain.

verify.title: Anyone should be able to rebuild a chunk from public inputs.
verify.body: This demo uses the same world generator and chunk renderer used by the playable world. Enter a seed and chunk coordinate to see the terrain the client builds from those public inputs.

demo.title: Main Game Chunk
demo.controls: Drag to rotate. Wheel or pinch to zoom.

seed.title: Public input contract
seed.body: The verifier starts from values that can be shown to players: seed, chunk coordinate, and generation version.

chain.title: Future on-chain commitment
chain.body: A future chain record can store the commitment hash, while clients and explorers recompute the chunk locally.

mining.title: Resource discovery should explain why this result happened.
mining.body: The live alpha can evolve, but the target model is simple: every important economic result should be derived from explicit inputs and produce a checkable hash path.

roll.title: Hash-derived roll
roll.body: The roll is calculated from seed, block coordinate, chunk coordinate, and nonce. This makes the proof replayable.

limit.title: Alpha limitation
limit.body: Until settlement is fully verified on-chain, the website must label proof demos as reference logic rather than final economic settlement.

status.title: Fairness claims are tracked by implementation status.

world.verification: Public seed, public rules, local recomputation, future chain commitment.

mining.verification: Hash-derived roll demo, planned settlement verification.

forging.verification: Recipe inputs, durability, output metadata, future mint records.

watchers.verification: No custody, no player transaction signing, region availability role only.

governance.verification: Versioned changelog, separated UI/world/economy/chain change categories.

boundaries.title: Honest fairness pages disclose what is not final yet.
boundaries.body: NiceChunk should avoid pretending that every alpha system is already fully decentralized. The stronger claim is to publish the path from client logic to verifiable protocol state.

client.title: Client
client.body: Renders the world, predicts movement, shows previews, and can recompute public generation logic.

wallet.title: Wallet
wallet.body: Signs player intent directly. Player assets should not depend on hidden project-controlled signing.

watcher.title: Guardian
watcher.body: Helps regions stay reachable and communicate. Guardians do not custody assets or sign player transactions.

chain.title: Solana
chain.body: The target settlement layer for critical identity, asset, commitment, and economy state.
